name: Problem Decomposition
instructions: You will answer 5 problem decomposition questions. Some ask you to arrange steps in the correct order; others ask you to write a decomposition plan. Timers are running but hidden — you will receive a warning when time is almost up. You cannot go back to previous questions.

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Arrange the following steps in the correct order to relocate a 200-person office to a new building with minimal disruption.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A mid-size company (200 employees) is relocating its single office to a new building across town. The move must happen over a weekend to minimize business disruption. IT infrastructure, furniture, and employee seating assignments all need to transfer.

items.text: [PLACEHOLDER] Step: Assess current office space and new location requirements
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Create detailed floor plan and seating assignments for new location
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Coordinate with IT to plan infrastructure migration and network setup
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Communicate move timeline and logistics to all employees
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Pack and label department equipment and materials
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Execute physical move of furniture and equipment over the weekend
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Verify IT systems, access badges, and workspace readiness on Monday morning

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Arrange the following steps in the correct order to add a new menu item at a restaurant that requires sourcing from a new supplier.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A popular restaurant wants to add a seasonal dish that requires an ingredient not currently in their supply chain. They need to find a reliable supplier, test the dish, train kitchen staff, and launch it to customers while maintaining food safety and quality standards.

items.text: [PLACEHOLDER] Step: Research and identify potential suppliers for the new ingredient
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Request samples and evaluate ingredient quality from shortlisted suppliers
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Negotiate pricing, delivery schedule, and minimum order quantities
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Develop and test the recipe with the chef team
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Conduct food safety and allergen assessment for the new dish
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Train kitchen and front-of-house staff on preparation and presentation
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Launch the menu item with a soft rollout and gather customer feedback

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Arrange the following steps in the correct order to transition a hospital's patient intake process from paper forms to a digital system while maintaining patient privacy compliance.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A regional hospital currently uses paper-based patient intake forms across its emergency, outpatient, and specialist departments. They must transition to a digital system that complies with healthcare privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA). The transition must not disrupt patient care during the changeover period.

items.text: [PLACEHOLDER] Step: Audit current paper intake workflow and identify all data fields collected
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Map privacy and compliance requirements for digital patient data handling
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Evaluate and select a digital intake platform that meets compliance standards
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Design digital forms mirroring current data collection with privacy safeguards
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Conduct a pilot in one department with parallel paper and digital intake
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Train all intake staff and address issues identified during the pilot
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Roll out digital intake across all departments with paper backup available
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Decommission paper forms after confirming digital system stability and compliance audit

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Arrange the following steps in the correct order to plan and execute a corporate conference for 500 attendees.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A company is organizing its annual corporate conference for 500 employees across multiple offices. The event includes keynote speakers, breakout sessions, catering, and AV requirements. It must be planned within a fixed budget and a 4-month timeline.

items.text: [PLACEHOLDER] Step: Define conference objectives, budget, and target attendance
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Select and book venue with required capacity and AV capabilities
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Confirm keynote speakers and plan breakout session topics
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Arrange catering, transportation, and accommodation logistics
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Send invitations and open registration for attendees
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Conduct final walkthrough and rehearsal at the venue
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Execute the conference and collect attendee feedback post-event

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Arrange the following steps in the correct order to unify customer accounts after a bank merger.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] Two regional banks are merging. Each has its own core banking system, account numbering scheme, and customer database. Customers of both banks must end up with unified accounts accessible through a single online banking platform. Regulatory requirements mandate uninterrupted access to funds throughout the transition.

items.text: [PLACEHOLDER] Step: Inventory both banks' account types, numbering schemes, and data formats
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Define the unified account structure and mapping rules between legacy systems
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Obtain regulatory approval for the account migration plan
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Build and test the data migration pipeline with synthetic account data
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Communicate migration timeline and changes to all affected customers
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Execute phased account migration with parallel system access during transition
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Validate migrated accounts and reconcile balances against source systems
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Decommission legacy systems after confirmation period and final audit

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Decompose the launch of a new customer loyalty program for a retail chain with 150 physical stores and an online store. Describe the key phases, dependencies, and potential risks.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A national retail chain with 150 brick-and-mortar stores and an e-commerce platform wants to launch a unified loyalty program. Customers should earn and redeem points both in-store and online. The chain currently has no loyalty program and uses separate POS and e-commerce systems.

criteria: [PLACEHOLDER] Identifies key phases including system integration, staff training, and customer communication
[PLACEHOLDER] Recognizes dependencies between POS integration, e-commerce integration, and unified customer database
[PLACEHOLDER] Addresses risks such as data synchronization failures, customer confusion, and staff adoption challenges

levels.label: Exceptional
levels.description: Comprehensive, well-structured, identifies non-obvious issues/steps. Shows systems thinking.

levels.label: Strong
levels.description: Covers key points with good structure. May miss one subtle element but demonstrates clear reasoning.

levels.label: Adequate
levels.description: Identifies obvious issues/steps but lacks depth or misses important considerations.

levels.label: Weak
levels.description: Superficial or partially correct. Shows some reasoning but significant gaps.

levels.label: Poor
levels.description: Misunderstands the task, provides irrelevant response, or demonstrates no structured thinking.

levels.label: No Response
levels.description: Blank or completely off-topic.

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Decompose the replacement of a legacy time-off request system for a company with 300 employees across 4 offices. Outline the phases, key decisions, and transition plan.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A company with 300 employees spread across 4 office locations currently manages time-off requests via email and shared spreadsheets. Different offices have different informal processes. Management wants a unified digital system that integrates with payroll. The transition must not cause any employees to lose accrued PTO data.

criteria: [PLACEHOLDER] Identifies need to audit and reconcile existing PTO data across all offices before migration
[PLACEHOLDER] Addresses the challenge of unifying different informal processes into a single standardized workflow
[PLACEHOLDER] Plans for payroll integration, parallel running period, and employee training across multiple locations

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Decompose the unification of inventory management across 3 warehouses that currently use different tracking systems. Describe how you would plan and sequence the work, including data migration and operational continuity.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A distribution company operates 3 warehouses in different regions. Warehouse A uses a commercial WMS, Warehouse B uses a custom-built Access database, and Warehouse C tracks inventory in spreadsheets. Management wants a single unified inventory system. The company ships 2,000+ orders daily and cannot afford significant downtime. Products overlap across warehouses, but SKU formats and naming conventions differ.

criteria: [PLACEHOLDER] Addresses SKU normalization and master data reconciliation as a prerequisite to migration
[PLACEHOLDER] Plans for phased warehouse-by-warehouse rollout to minimize operational risk
[PLACEHOLDER] Identifies data quality challenges across disparate systems and proposes validation strategies

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Decompose the centralization of student enrollment across a school district with 20 schools, given a 5-month deadline. Outline the phases, critical dependencies, and how you would handle the constraints.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A school district with 20 schools (12 elementary, 5 middle, 3 high) currently handles enrollment independently at each school using a mix of paper forms and local databases. The district superintendent has mandated a centralized online enrollment system to be operational before the next enrollment period in 5 months. Each school has different data fields, some families have children in multiple schools, and the district must comply with state student data privacy regulations.

criteria: [PLACEHOLDER] Recognizes the tight timeline constraint and structures phases to meet the 5-month deadline
[PLACEHOLDER] Addresses data standardization across 20 schools with different data fields and formats
[PLACEHOLDER] Considers multi-school families, student data privacy compliance, and stakeholder change management

questions.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Arrange the following steps in the correct order to transition a manufacturing company from a single supplier to a dual-supplier model for a critical component.
questions.context: [PLACEHOLDER] A manufacturing company currently sources a critical component from a single supplier. To reduce supply chain risk, management has decided to qualify and onboard a second supplier. Production cannot be interrupted during the transition, and the new supplier's components must meet the same quality and specification standards.

items.text: [PLACEHOLDER] Step: Document current component specifications and quality acceptance criteria
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Identify and evaluate candidate second suppliers against specifications
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Request and test sample components from the top candidate supplier
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Negotiate contract terms including pricing, lead times, and quality guarantees
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Run a small production batch using the new supplier's components alongside existing supply
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Validate production output quality and adjust processes if needed
[PLACEHOLDER] Step: Establish ongoing dual-supplier ordering schedule and monitoring process
